ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Manila clams are ideal — smaller and more tender. Littleneck clams work but need ~5 extra minutes.
items: 2 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for serving
4 slices thick-cut sourdough bread
4 garlic cloves — 2 halved, 2 smashed
3 oz thinly sliced prosciutto, torn
2 tbsp fresh thyme leaves
1 tbsp chopped fresh oregano
1 cup dry white wine (pinot grigio or Sauvignon Blanc)
Kosher salt & freshly ground pepper
2 lbs Manila clams (see note above)
Crushed red pepper flakes, for serving
Fresh basil leaves, for serving
ingredients.callout: No wine? Substitute an equal amount of chicken broth. The broth still carries the garlic and herb flavor beautifully.

body.html: Garlicky toasted sourdough piled high with Manila clams steamed open in a white wine broth with thyme, oregano, and crispy prosciutto. Fancy-looking, 30-minute weeknight dinner.

items: Toast sourdough in olive oil, rub with raw garlic
Crisp prosciutto in the same skillet
Build the broth — smashed garlic, thyme, oregano, wine
Steam clams until open and broth reduces by a third
Spoon over toast, crumble prosciutto on top, finish with basil

body.html: One skillet, one pan. The prosciutto crisps in olive oil, then the broth builds right in the same skillet — all that rendered fat goes into the sauce.

slides.title: Toast the Bread
slides.checkboxLabel: Bread toasted and rubbed with garlic

items: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
When the oil shimmers, add the bread slices.
Cook until golden and toasted, 1–2 minutes per side.
Remove the bread from the skillet.
Immediately rub each slice with the cut side of a halved garlic clove.
Set toast aside on plates.

body.html: Garlic rub trick: The raw cut garlic melts into the hot, oily toast — no need to mince. Do it while the bread is still hot.

items: Using the same skillet over medium heat, lay in the prosciutto.
Cook without moving until crisp, 2–3 minutes per side.
Remove and set aside — it will crisp up further as it cools.
Leave the rendered fat in the skillet — it's flavor.

body.html: Don't rush it: Resist the urge to move the prosciutto. Let it sit flat so it crisps evenly rather than curling and steaming.

items: In the same skillet, add smashed garlic, thyme, and oregano.
Cook over medium heat until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in the wine, season with salt and pepper.
Bring to a simmer over high heat.
Add the clams, cover, and cook until clams open and sauce reduces by one-third — 8–10 minutes.
Discard any clams that don't open after 10 minutes.

body.html: Littleneck clams: Need about 5 extra minutes to open up. Keep the heat steady and lid on.

items: Divide the garlic toast between two wide bowls or plates.
Spoon the clams and wine broth generously over the top.
Crumble the crisped prosciutto over the clams.
Finish with crushed red pepper flakes, fresh basil, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Serve immediately with extra bread to sop up the broth.

body.html: Leftovers: Store any remaining sauce (without the toast) refrigerated in an airtight container for 1–2 days. The clams don't reheat well — eat those fresh.
